sources: MEXC official fee schedule (0% maker / 0.10% taker spot promotion, active since Q4 2022)
Academic: Foucault et al. (2023) crypto execution cost models
Industry: Kaiko spread/depth data benchmarks for CEX
assumptions: MEXC 0% maker promotion assumed to continue (has been active 3+ years)
Spread model: half-spread ~ k / sqrt(daily_volume_usd), k=150 calibrated to CEX benchmarks
Slippage model: market impact ~ sigma * sqrt(trade_size / daily_volume), for $200 per-trade size
Trade size assumption: $200 per trade (10% of $2,000 account)
T1 daily volume: ~$7.7M median (from 4H bar median * 6)
T2 daily volume: ~$269K median (from 4H bar median * 6)
Conservative: using P50 volume (half of coins in tier are worse)

mexc_fees.fee_tier: Base tier (no volume requirements for spot)
mexc_fees.note: MEXC has run 0% maker fee promotion since Q4 2022. Taker fee is flat 0.10% (10bps) for all spot pairs at base tier. MX token holding gives 20% discount on taker fees.

spread_slippage_model.methodology: CEX spread model: half_spread_bps = k / sqrt(daily_vol_usd) * 10000, where k=150 (calibrated to Kaiko CEX benchmarks). Slippage: market_impact_bps = sigma_daily * sqrt(trade_size / daily_vol) * 10000, sigma_daily ~ 5% for crypto. Trade size = $200.
spread_slippage_model.calibration_note: k=150 yields ~1.7bps half-spread for BTC ($5B daily vol), ~5bps for mid-caps ($1M daily), consistent with published CEX spread data. This is for MEXC specifically which is known for competitive spreads due to market-maker incentives (0% maker fee).

tier1.note: All-in = exchange_fee + spread + slippage per side

tier2.note: All-in = exchange_fee + spread + slippage per side

vs_kraken.tier1_pct_cheaper_taker: MEXC is 60% cheaper than Kraken for T1 taker
vs_kraken.tier2_pct_cheaper_taker: MEXC is 58% cheaper than Kraken for T2 taker

implications.backtest_recalibration: Current Kraken-calibrated costs are 2-2.5x higher than MEXC reality. H20 VWAP_DEVIATION with PF=0.90 at Kraken fees would likely show PF>1.0 at MEXC fees.
implications.maker_vs_taker: 0% maker fee is the dominant factor. If the strategy can use limit orders (even partially), costs drop dramatically. At 50% maker fill rate, effective T1 cost = ~7.5 bps, T2 cost = ~18.5 bps.
implications.caveat_promotion: The 0% maker fee is a promotional rate. If MEXC reverts to standard fees (e.g., 0.10% maker / 0.10% taker), costs would increase by ~10bps per side for all maker orders.
implications.caveat_model_vs_reality: Spread/slippage estimates are model-based, not measured from live order book data. Real-world execution may differ, especially during volatile periods or for the lowest-volume T2 coins.
implications.recommendation: Re-run the screening pipeline with MEXC-calibrated costs: T1=13bps/side (taker, conservative), T2=24bps/side (taker, conservative). Also test maker scenario: T1=3bps, T2=14bps.
